vocab.meaning: người rất khéo tay hoặc giỏi về một việc gì đó
vocab.example: My art teacher always said I was a dab hand at sketching portraits, even though I struggled with landscapes.

vocab.meaning: mô tả hoặc làm gì đó một cách khái quát, thiếu chi tiết
vocab.example: When I was a kid, I used to paint with a broad brush, focusing on big splashes of color rather than intricate details.

vocab.meaning: đẹp hoàn hảo như trong tranh
vocab.example: I remember trying to make my drawings look picture-perfect, but they usually ended up covered in messy thumbprints.

vocab.meaning: một tờ giấy trắng/khởi đầu mới đầy tiềm năng (chưa bị ảnh hưởng bởi cái cũ)
vocab.example: Entering the art room felt like being given a blank canvas where I could be whoever I wanted to be for an hour.

vocab.meaning: kỳ quặc, lạ lùng hoặc rất khác biệt (thường dùng cho phong cách nghệ thuật)
vocab.example: Even as a child, my painting style was a bit off the wall, often featuring purple trees and orange skies.

vocab.meaning: làm cho cái gì đó trở nên sống động/có hồn
vocab.example: It was magical to see how a few strokes of a brush could bring a dull sketch to life.
I can still see my childhood bedroom in my mind's eye, exactly as I used to draw it in my old sketchbook.

vocab.meaning: chìm đắm hoàn toàn vào một trạng thái hay công việc
vocab.example: I would often sink into a creative trance for hours, completely oblivious to the world around me while I painted.

vocab.meaning: đạt được mục tiêu/thành công như mong đợi
vocab.example: Not every drawing hit the mark, but the process of creating something from scratch was always fulfilling.

vocab.meaning: tạo ra nét riêng/phong cách cá nhân cho cái gì đó
vocab.example: Our teacher encouraged us not to copy the masters but to put our own spin on every piece we produced.
